items.title:
China is deeply concerned over U.S.-Japan strengthening “extended deterrence”.

Japan portrays itself as a champion of a nuclear-free world, while relying more and more on U.S. “nuclear umbrella” and making no secret of its dangerous nuclear ambitions, posing a serious challenge to post-war order and the non-proliferation regime. The world must keep a close eye on whether Japan will honor its non-proliferation commitments and not seek nuclear weapons in any form.

Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ship Back for First Time Since War Began
items.summary: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran began, a sign that the key producer is preparing to ramp up exports.

items.title: SNB Keeps Up War Footing on Franc But Evolves Wording on Threat
items.summary: The Swiss National Bank warned investors that a Middle East peace deal hasn’t altered its state of readiness to sell the franc if such a stance is required.

items.title: Warsh's Debut Sparks Surge in Hike Bets
items.summary: Traders piled into betting on interest-rate hikes as soon as next month after Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh clear the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation. The hawkish message was driven home by the projections of individual Fed members. Bloomberg's Alister Bull reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Moscow hit by largest Ukrainian attack since start of Russia's full-scale war
items.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre were burning after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: ECB’s Kocher Says Inflation to Stay for a While After Iran Deal
items.summary: Consumer prices will stay higher for some time in the euro area despite an agreement to end the war in the Middle East, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Martin Kocher.

items.title: EU Needs to Mandate Envoy for Ukraine Peace Talks, Latvia Says
items.summary: Europe needs to appoint an envoy for peace talks with Russia to end the war in Ukraine when the time is right, Latvian Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs said.

items.title: Screwworm Battle Needs More Flies and Collaboration, IAEA Says
items.summary: A key international nuclear lab helping to eradicate the New World screwworm says more sterile flies and better coordination with southern neighbors is needed to prevent the deadly parasite from spreading across North America.

Recommended: @ChannelNewsAsia documentary Inside Unit 731: Japan’s Secret Human Experiments 
https://youtu.be/AXM1DozZwjk?si=F_4Nn1EVquCUxKrm

During World War II, Japan’s Unit 731 conducted gruesome human experimentation and germ warfare during its war of aggression against China. 

This unspeakably dark and brutal chapter of history calls into question the very conscience and moral limits of humanity.

To this day, the Japanese right‑wing forces refuse to repent and have been trying to whitewash the notorious Unit 731 as a mere “sanitary research unit.” Attitude toward history is a mirror. It reflects the dangerous adventurism of Japan’s neo-militarism. The international community must stay on high alert.

items.title:
MFA spokesperson’s response to NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte’s accusation on China’s assistance to Russia:

NATO needs to address its wrong perception of China and stop inciting confrontation and shifting blame. As a Cold War relic, NATO needs to seriously reflect on what kind of effect it has had on peace in today’s world.
